question: Where is the Brahmaputra Cracker and Polymer Limited Industry located?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Brahmaputra Cracker and Polymer Limited (BCPL) has set up a Petrochemical Complex at Lepetkata. The complex is spread over 3000 Bighas of land at Lepetkata, and is situated at approximately 15 Kilometres away from the Tea City Dibrugarh, Assam.

question: Which of the following district of Assam has the highest concentration of forest?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B No explanation is given for this question Let's Discuss on Board

question: Which one of the following was the first capital of Ahom Kingdom in Assam?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Charaideo or Che-Rai-Doi is a town in Charaideo district, Assam, India and was also the first capital of the Ahom kingdom established by the first Ahom king Chao Lung Siu-Ka-Pha in 1253.

question: Who was hanged with Maniram, Pioli Phukan and Piyali Barua?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Maniram Dewan and Piyali Barua were publicly hanged on 26th February 1858 at the Jorhat Central Jail. Maniram's death was widely mourned in Assam and several tea gaden workers struck work to express their support for the rebellion.

question: The famous book 'Assamese- Its Formation and Development' was written by
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Assamese its Formation and Development is a Scientific Treatise on the History and Philology of the Assamese Language written by Dr. Banikanta Kakati.

question: Who has written the first Assamese romantic poem Bon Kunwori?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Chandra Kumar Agarwala was an eminent writer, poet, journalist from Assam. He is a pioneer people of Jonaki Era, the age of romanticism of Assamese literature. He wrote the first Assamese romantic poem 'Bon Kunwori'.

question: The first public sector oil refinery in India is situated at?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Digboi is known as the Oil City of Assam where the first oil well in Asia was drilled. The first refinery was started here as early as 1901. Digboi has the oldest oil well in operation.

question: Which one of the following cities is also known as the 'Manchester of Assam'?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Sualkuchi is a census town in Kamrup district in the Indian state of Assam. It is situated on the north bank of the river Brahmaputra, about 35 km from Guwahati, Sualkuchi is a block of Kamrup District. It has large number of cottage industry engaged in handloom, for which it is also known as the "Manchester of Assam".

question: The river Subansiri completely enters into Assam at which of the following places?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: The Subansiri is the second largest river in Assam. After flowing for 190 km through Tibet, it enters India. It continues its journey through the Himalaya of India for 200 km and enters into the plains of Assam through a gorge near Gerukamukh.

question: Which of the following districts of Assam has been declared as first tobacco free district?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Jorhat has been declared Assam's first smoke-free district and will be showcased as a model district in the country for the National Tobacco Control Programme (NTCP).

question: Which one of the following district of Assam shares the longest international boundary?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: The Dhubri district has longest international border in Assam state of India. The length of the India-Bangladesh International border in Dhubri district is about 134 km. Karimganj has 92 km long India-Bangladesh international border. Total length of India-Bangladesh border in Assam state of India is 263 km.

question: Which river makes an inter-state boundary between Assam and Tripura?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B No explanation is given for this question Let's Discuss on Board

question: The only Assamese to become the President of Indian National Congress is
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Deva Kanta Barua was an Indian politician from Assam, who served as the President of the Indian National Congress during the Emergency (1975 - 77).

question: In 1930 the first private college was established, which one of the following is that
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Jagannath Barooah College (popularly known as J. B. College) is an autonomous college located in Jorhat, Assam, India. It was founded on 19th August 1930. The College is affiliated to Dibrugarh University and recognized by University Grants Commission (UGC). It was accredited by National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC), India in 2003.

question: In which of the following movements Kanaklata Barua became a martyr?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Kanaklata Barua, also called Birbala and Shaheed (martyr), was an Indian independence activist and AISF leader who was shot dead by the Indian Imperial Police of the British Raj while leading a procession bearing the National Flag during the Quit India Movement of 1942.

question: The Kamakhya Temple is located on which Hill top?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: The Kamakhya Temple is located at the top of the Nilanchal Hills at an altitude of 800 feet above the sea level, in the state of Assam, in India. The Kamakhya Temple situated in the guwahati city is an important place in the tourism map of North-East India.

question: In which of the following cities 'Gibbon Wildlife Sanctuary' is located?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B No explanation is given for this question Let's Discuss on Board

question: Which tribe of Assam celebrates an agro-based festival 'Ali-Aai-Ligang'?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Most of the tribal festivals celebrated in Assam are springtime festivals related to the cultivation or harvest. The Festival of Ali-Aai-Ligang in Assam is a spring festival celebrated by the Mishing Tribe of people. It especially began with Ahu paddy cultivation.

question: Who was the first Assamese to receive the 'Sahitya Academy Award'?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Jatindranath Duarah was the first Assamese to win Sahitya Academy award. Jatindranath Dowara is one of the eminent Assamese poets. He is popularly known as Bonphulor Kobi in Assam. In 1955, Jatindranath Dowara was awarded with the prestigious Sahitya Akademi Award for his book Bon Phul (poetry).

question: In 640 AD. Chinese traveller Hiuen Tsang visited Assam (Kamrupa). The king who gave him warm reception was
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Chinese pilgrim Hiuen-Tsang visited Kamrupa in 640 AD. He met Kamrupa King Bhaskara Barmana in Gauda and got an invitation to visit his kingdom. So, Hiuen-Tsang came to Assam and attended the court of King Bhaskara Barmana. His accounts on Kamrupa are valuable sources of the history of Assam.

question: In Assamese society the custom of 'Suwaguritola' is associated with
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D No explanation is given for this question Let's Discuss on Board

question: Under the Assam Panchayat Act, 1994 which institution is excluded from 'Panchyati Raj Bodies'?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C No explanation is given for this question Let's Discuss on Board

question: The Assamese writer who is also known as 'Bokulbonor Kobi' is
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Ananda Chandra Barua was a writer, poet, playwright, translator, journalist and actor from Assam. He is popularly known as Bokulbonor Kobi in Assamese literacy society.

question: The total area of Assam is
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Assam is a state in northeastern India, south of the eastern Himalayas along the Brahmaputra and Barak River valleys. Assam covers an area of 78,438 km2 (30,285 sq mi). The state is bordered by Bhutan and Arunachal Pradesh to the north; Nagaland and Manipur to the east; Meghalaya, Tripura, Mizoram and Bangladesh to the south and West Bengal to the west via the Siliguri Corridor, a 22 kilometers (14 mi) wide strip of land that connects the state to the rest of India. Assamese and Bodo are the official languages of Assam, while Bengali is official in the Barak Valley.

question: When was the historic Assam Accord signed?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: The Assam Accord was signed on 15th August, 1985 amongst Union of India, Government of Assam, All Assam Student Union, All Assam Gana Sangram Parishad. To implement the various Clauses of Assam Accord a new Department has been established in the name of “Implementation of Assam Accord Department” during the year 1986.

question: In which of the following year the Ahom invasion of Assam took place?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D No explanation is given for this question Let's Discuss on Board

question: The Assam Police Day is observed on
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: The Assam Police Day is observed in remembrance of IPS K. R. Choudhary, who took over as Assam Police Chief on 1st October in 1951.

question: In which year Bhupen Hazarika was awarded with Dadasaheb Phalke Award?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Bhupen Hazarika received the National Film Award for Best Music Direction in 1975, the Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (1987), Padma Shri (1977) and Padma Bhushan (2001), Dada Saheb Phalke Award (1992), the highest award for cinema in India and Sangeet Natak Akademi Fellowship (2008), the highest award of the Sangeet Natak Akademi.

question: The first One Day International Cricket match was played in Assam in which year?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: The first One day International Cricket (ODI) match in Assam state of India was played on 17th December, 1983. It was played between India and West Indies. The venue was Nehru Stadium, Guwahati. West Indies won the ODI cricket match by 6 wickets.

question: The Guwahati Stock Exchange was established in Assam in which of the following years?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Guwahati Stock Exchange (GSE) is a defunct stock exchange located in Gauhati, Assam. It was incorporated on 29th November 1983 and it was recognised by the Government of India on 1st May 1984 and was permitted to be closed by market regulator SEBI in January 2015.

question: Assam State Electricity Board (ASEB) was constituted in
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: The Assam State Electricity Board was constituted with effect from 1st June 1958 and with the appointment of Shri G. Goswami as Chief Engineer of the A.S.E.B., the post of Electrical Adviser was abolished.

question: In which year Panchayati Raj was formally introduced in Assam?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B No explanation is given for this question Let's Discuss on Board

question: The Bodo Sahitya Sabha was founded in which year?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: The Bodo Sahitya Sabha promotes the Bodo language and Bodo literature. It was founded under the presidency and leadership of Joy Bhadra Hagjer, at Basugaon, in the district of Kokrajhar, Assam on 16th November 1952. It consisted of representatives of Assam, West Bengal, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Tripura and Nepal in abroad.

question: In which of the following year the Guwahati University was founded?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Guwahati University was incorporated by an Act of 1947. It started functioning on 26th January, 1948 as an affiliating, teaching and residential university in a few temporary buildings in Guwahati, with K. K. Handique as the first Vice-Chancellor.

question: In which year Assam Pradesh Congress Committee was founded?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Assam Pradesh Congress Committee (or Assam PCC) was formed in June 1921. Its headquarters is at Rajiv Bhavan in G. S. Road, Guwahati.

question: In which year Patharughat Revolt took place?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Patharighat, previously known as Patharughat, is a place in Assam, India. It is one of the revenue circles in Darrang district. The place is famous for the event of 1894, popularly known as Patharughatar Ran (Battle of Patharughat).

question: In which year Chief Commissionership was introduced in Assam?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: On 6th February 1874, Assam, including Sylhet, was severed from Bengal to form the Assam Chief-Commissionership, also known as the 'North-East Frontier'. Shillong was chosen as the capital of the Non-Regulation Province of Assam in September 1874. The Lushai Hills were transferred to Assam in 1897.

question: In which year the Assam region was separated from the Bengal Presidency as North-East Frontier?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: At the end of the First Burmese war in 1826, the whole of Assam gradually came under the British rule. In 1874, Assam was detached from the administrative control of Bengal and was made into a separate Chief Commissionership.

question: In which year the first newspaper of Assam 'Orunodoi' was published?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: A monthly news magazine called Orunodoi, a first for Assam was published from Sivasagar in January 1846, with an American Baptist missionary, Nathan Brown, as its first editor. The publication ushered in a new era in Assamese journalism and literature.

question: In which year the first Printing Press was established in Assam?
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D No explanation is given for this question Let's Discuss on Board

question: The number of wildlife sanctuaries in Assam is
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Assam, known for its rich biodiversity and varied ecosystems, is home to 17 wildlife sanctuaries that play an essential role in the conservation of flora and fauna. Below is a list of these sanctuaries: 1. Kaziranga Wildlife Sanctuary Famous for its population of one-horned rhinoceroses, it is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. 2. Manas Wildlife Sanctuary Another UNESCO World Heritage Site, it is known for its rich biodiversity, including tigers, elephants, and the golden langur. 3. Pobitora Wildlife Sanctuary Known for its population of one-horned rhinoceroses, it is located on the southern banks of the Brahmaputra. 4. Dibru-Saikhowa Wildlife Sanctuary A hotspot for biodiversity, this sanctuary is known for its vast wetlands and the rare white-winged wood duck. 5. Nameri Wildlife Sanctuary Situated in the foothills of the Eastern Himalayas, it is home to many species of birds, including the rare white-winged wood duck. 6. Bura Chapori Wildlife Sanctuary Located on the banks of the Brahmaputra River, it is known for its wetlands and the presence of tigers and rhinoceroses. 7. Kajiranga National Park A UNESCO World Heritage Site, it is famous for its large population of the one-horned rhinoceros. 8. Udhwa Wetland Wildlife Sanctuary Known for its wetlands, this sanctuary is an important site for migratory birds. 9. Chakrashila Wildlife Sanctuary This sanctuary is famous for its biodiversity and is home to the golden langur, a species endemic to Assam. 10. Gibbon Wildlife Sanctuary Known for its population of hoolock gibbons, the only ape species found in India. 11. Kanglang Wildlife Sanctuary Located near the foothills of the Eastern Himalayas, it is home to a variety of wildlife, including elephants and tigers. 12. Kheroni Wildlife Sanctuary This sanctuary is known for its diverse plant and animal species, providing a rich habitat for many wildlife species. 13. Laokhowa Wildlife Sanctuary Situated on the banks of the Brahmaputra, this sanctuary is known for its one-horned rhinoceros and various bird species. 14. Sila Wildlife Sanctuary Located in the foothills of the Karbi Anglong district, it is known for its biodiversity and beautiful landscapes. 15. Bhalukpong Wildlife Sanctuary Known for its rich flora and fauna, this sanctuary lies in the foothills of the Eastern Himalayas and is part of the larger Bhalukpong ecosystem. 16. Dikhari Wildlife Sanctuary Situated along the Brahmaputra, it is known for its rich biodiversity and role in preserving endangered species. 17. Barail Wildlife Sanctuary A rich forest reserve, it is home to numerous species of flora and fauna, including tigers, elephants, and various bird species. These wildlife sanctuaries in Assam form an integral part of India's natural heritage and contribute significantly to wildlife conservation efforts. They are also prime destinations for eco-tourism, attracting wildlife enthusiasts and nature lovers from around the world.

question: The state of Assam has ............ representatives in the Lok Sabha.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: The state of Assam has 14 representatives in the Lok Sabha.

question: The state of Assam has ........ representatives in the Rajya Sabha.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: The state of Assam has 7 representatives in the Rajya Sabha.

question: According to 2011 Census the literacy rate of Assam is
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: The total literacy rate of Assam is 72.19% according to the 2011 census study. The male literacy rate is 77.85% and the female literacy rate is 66.27% in Assam. As per the 2011 census, the total population of Assam was 31,169,272.
